# UC-17.1.70 · OCSP Responder and CRL Repository Reachability and Latency Monitoring

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Operations, Security &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*When a certificate is presented, ISE asks the issuing authority 'is this still valid?' over OCSP/CRL. If those services are down, ISE may silently approve or block everyone. We watch their health.*

---

## Description

Tracks OCSP responder availability and latency, and CRL repository download success/failure. Alerts when OCSP becomes unreachable, OCSP latency exceeds 2 seconds, or CRL downloads fail.

## Value

OCSP/CRL failures cause certificate revocation checks to fall back to a pre-configured behaviour (often 'allow' or 'use cached'). Either silently weakens authentication or causes mass auth failures depending on configuration. Visibility prevents both.

## Implementation

Schedule every 15 min.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Block OCSP responder URL via firewall. ISE emits 35102. UC-17.1.70 fires.

### Negative scenario

OCSP/CRL reachable. No fires.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (35102, 35201, 35103)
| stats count by ocsp_responder_url, crl_repository_url, MessageCode, failure_reason
| where (MessageCode=="35102" AND count > 5) OR (MessageCode=="35201" AND count > 1) OR (MessageCode=="35103" AND count > 0)
```

## Visualization

OCSP responder availability heatmap; CRL freshness by repository; latency distribution.

## Known False Positives

- Brief OCSP responder maintenance windows.
- Network blips affecting external OCSP responders.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — Certificate Revocation](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/security/ise/3-4/admin_guide/b_ise_admin_3_4/m_basic_setup.html)
